description: Create .editorconfig for consistent formatting across editors (indent_style=space, indent_size=4 for Rust, charset=utf-8, trim_trailing_whitespace, insert_final_newline). Create deny.toml for cargo-deny: configure advisories (deny unmaintained, deny yanked), license policy (allow MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD-*, deny copyleft), and bans section. These policy files enforce supply-chain hygiene from day one.

title: justfile with common dev targets
description: Create justfile with targets for every common task: 'just fmt', 'just lint' (clippy --workspace --all-targets -- -D warnings), 'just test' (nextest run --workspace), 'just doc-test' (test --doc --workspace), 'just deny' (cargo deny check), 'just ci' (runs all quality gates in sequence), 'just dist' (placeholder for release builds). The plan explicitly calls for just targets for every common task to make the repo agent-friendly. Agents and humans should use the same commands.

title: Milestone 4: Release Hardening
description: Set up release-plz automation, cargo-dist artifacts, checksums, attestations, Dependabot, and cargo-deny. Exit criteria: tagged release produces installable artifacts for all primary platforms.

title: release-plz setup (versioning + changelog automation)
description: Configure release-plz for semantic versioning, release PRs, and changelog automation. Create .github/workflows/release-pr.yml that: opens/updates release PRs, bumps versions according to conventional commits, updates CHANGELOG.md, creates Git tags and GitHub Releases after merge. Use Conventional Commits format. Start at 0.x until recovery flow and CLI contract settle. Minor bumps for features, patch bumps for fixes. The plan says: 'Major 1.0 only after the recovery flow is proven stable and the machine interface is intentionally versioned.'

title: cargo-dist setup (cross-platform release artifacts)
description: Configure cargo-dist to build release artifacts. Create .github/workflows/dist.yml. Target platforms: x86_64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-pc-windows-msvc, x86_64-apple-darwin, aarch64-apple-darwin. Optional later: aarch64-unknown-linux-gnu, x86_64-unknown-linux-musl. Artifacts: archives (tar.gz for Linux/macOS, zip for Windows), SHA-256 checksums, optional shell/PowerShell installer scripts. Integrate with GitHub Releases so each tagged version gets downloadable binaries.

title: Checksums and provenance attestations
description: Generate SHA-256 checksums for all release artifacts and publish alongside binaries. Set up GitHub artifact attestations using actions/attest-build-provenance for supply-chain transparency. This lets users verify: (1) integrity via checksums, (2) provenance via attestations (artifact was built by this repo's CI, not tampered). The plan says: 'Generate attestations for release artifacts' as part of the security and supply chain requirements.

title: First public prerelease (0.1.0)
description: Cut the first public prerelease after hardware validation succeeds. Tag as v0.1.0. Publish release artifacts to GitHub Releases. Optionally enable crates.io trusted publishing for cargo install - but only if the CLI contract has been declared ready. The plan says: 'cargo install is enabled only if the CLI contract has been declared ready for public installation.' This is the project's public debut - make sure README, CHANGELOG, and documentation are polished.

comments.author: ynezz
comments.text: The 0.1.0 prerelease is the project's first public artifact. Before tagging: (1) README must have a human-driven e2e recovery example, a machine-driven --json example, and a stop-at-uboot example, (2) CHANGELOG must describe what works and what doesn't, (3) exit codes must be documented, (4) JSON event schema must be documented or at least versioned. Versioning policy: 0.x until the recovery flow and CLI contract settle. The plan explicitly defers crates.io publishing until the CLI contract is declared ready.

title: Dependabot configuration
description: Create .github/dependabot.yml to enable automated dependency updates for both GitHub Actions (weekly) and Cargo dependencies (weekly). The plan requires this as part of supply-chain security. Pin Actions by full SHA in workflows, then rely on Dependabot to propose SHA bumps when new versions release. For Cargo deps, Dependabot PRs go through the full CI quality gate before merge.

title: cargo-deny advisory and license policy
description: Refine deny.toml with production-ready advisory and license policies. Advisory section: deny unmaintained crates, deny yanked crates, configure severity thresholds. License section: allow MIT/Apache-2.0/BSD variants, deny copyleft (GPL/LGPL/AGPL). Bans section: deny specific crates if needed. This builds on the initial deny.toml from M0 but hardens it for release readiness. Run cargo deny check as part of CI and pre-release checks.
